folio.label: 186r
folio.l: Fortune was first frend , and sithe a foo

note.notetxt: 637Cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: No man ne truste , vp oon hire fauour longe
But haue hire in a wayte , for euer mo
Wytnesse on alle , theise Conquerours st¼nge
¢ This riche Cresus , whilom kyngö of lyde
Of whiche , Cresus Cirus , sore hi~ dradde
ñet was he kaugút , a myddes al his pride
And to be brent , men to the fyr hi~ ladde
But sucú a reyn , doun fro the walken shadde
That it slow the fyr , & made to hi~ escape
But to be war , no grace ©et he hadde
Til Fortune , on the galwes , made hi~ gape
¢ Whan he eskaped was , he gan nat stynte
For to begynne , a newe werre a geyn
He wende wel , for æ fortune hi~ sente
Swicú hap~ , æ he eskaped thurgú the reyn
That of his foos , he mygúte nat be slayn
And eke a Sweuene , vp oon a nygút he mette
Of whicú he was so proud , & eke so fayn
That in vengeaunce , he al his herte sette
¢ Vp oon a tree he was , as æ hi~ thougúte
Where Iuppiter hi~ wessú , bothe bak & syde
And Phebus eke , a fair towayl hi~ brougúte
To drye hi~ witú , & her for wex his péde
And to his dougúter , æ stod hi~ beside
Whicú æ he knew , in hey Science habounde
He bad hire telle hi~ , what it signyfiede
And she his drem , bygan rigút thus expounde
¢ The tree q¶ she , the galwes is to mene
And Iuppiter bitokenetú , Snow & reyn
And Phebus , w his towayle so clene
Tho ben the Sonnes stremes , for to seyne
Thow shalt an hanged be , fader certeyne
Reyn shal the wasshe , & sonne shal the drye
Thus warned hi~ ful plat , & ful pleyne
His dougúter , whicú æ called was , Phanye
¢ An hanged was Cresus , the proude kyngö
His real trone , mygút hi~ nougút availle
Tragedies , noon other manä thyngö
Ne can in syngynge , crie ne bywaille
But for æ fortune , alday wol assaille
Witú vnwar Strok , the Regnes æ ben proude
For whan men trosten hire , than wyl she faille
